title: Motivation methods in practice: financial motivators, non-financial motivators
summary: 9609 AS — financial and non-financial motivators, job design, and recommending packages for case firms.
learningObjectives: Describe financial motivators: wages, commission, profit sharing.
Describe non-financial motivators: enrichment, empowerment, teamworking.
Evaluate suitability for different jobs and workers.
Recommend a motivation package using case evidence.

simpleExplanation.title: Pay and beyond
simpleExplanation.summary: Financial motivators link reward to performance; non-financial motivators improve the job itself and how people feel about work. Most effective packages combine both.
steps: Financial: salary, piece rate, commission, profit share, fringe benefits.
Commission links pay directly to sales performance.
Job enrichment adds responsibility and variety.
Teamworking and empowerment meet social and esteem needs.

flashcards.back: Pay per unit produced — Taylor; risk of quality sacrifice.
% of sales value — motivates sales staff; income variability.
Share of firm profits — aligns interests; delayed reward.
More planning, control, variety in same role — Herzberg motivator.
More tasks at same level — may reduce boredom.
Decision authority delegated to front-line staff.
Match methods to job type and workforce needs — not one-size-fits-all.

sections.content: **Motivation methods** (2.2.4) completes **Topic 2.2**. **Recommend** questions: pick **2 financial + 2 non-financial** methods justified by **theory and case**.

items: **Time-rate wage:** Stable income; no extra effort incentive.
**Performance-related pay:** Links to output/sales; may increase stress.
**Training & promotion:** Non-financial but valued by ambitious staff.
**Team bonuses:** Combine financial reward with social cohesion.

sections.question: Retail chain wants to motivate store staff to improve customer service without large permanent wage rises. Recommend a motivation package.
sections.solution:
**Financial (limited cost):**
- **Team monthly bonus** if store hits mystery-shopper score — shared success, small % of payroll.
- **Employee discount** — low marginal cost, valued fringe benefit.

**Non-financial:**
- **Empowerment** to resolve customer complaints up to $50 without manager — faster service, esteem.
- **Recognition scheme** — employee of the month, visible board (esteem, Mayo social).

**Evaluation:** Avoid pure **commission** on sales — may push hard selling and damage **customer orientation**.
